message:
perf(pager,core): lightweight cache metrics + Cow for InsertStatement rewrite

Two independent hot-path optimizations targeting the top pending hotspots
from the 2026-04-23 profiling hand-off
(tests/artifacts/perf/profiling-handoff-20260423T155542Z/):

T2 (score 5.0): ShardedPageCache::metrics_lightweight_snapshot
---------------------------------------------------------------
`refresh_eprocess_oracle` samples cache hit-ratio and pool-utilization
at ~1-of-64 statements. It was calling the full `metrics_snapshot()`
which iterates every resident PageSlot via `stable_snapshot`, allocates
a Vec<PageCachePageSnapshot>, iterates again for dirty_ratio_pct, and
locks the eviction-policy mutex to sample queue sizes. None of that data
is consumed by the oracle. Observed self-time: 5.08% at MT 8t, ~3% at 1t
in bd-m4s2c-20260423T013614Z.

- Added PageCacheLightweightSnapshot (6 fields: hits, misses, admits,
  evictions, cached_pages, pool_capacity) plus a matching
  ShardedPageCache::metrics_lightweight_snapshot() that skips slot
  iteration, Vec allocation, and the eviction-policy lock.
- Added Pager::cache_metrics_lightweight_snapshot() and the matching
  PagerBackend enum arm for Memory/IoUring/Unix/Windows backends.
- Rewired Connection::refresh_eprocess_oracle to consume the lightweight
  variant. The oracle's anomaly score is unchanged; only the sampling
  cost moves.

T7 (score 6.0): InsertStatement rewrite returns Cow instead of cloning
----------------------------------------------------------------------
`prepared_direct_simple_insert_plan` and `compile_table_insert` both
call `resolve_insert_values_subqueries`, which deep-cloned the full
`InsertStatement` even when the insert has no VALUES subqueries (the
99% case: `INSERT INTO t VALUES (1, 2, 3)`). Observed self-time:
1.69% @ 8t, 2.06% @ 16t in bd-m4s2c-20260423T013614Z.

- Changed return type to Result<Cow<'a, InsertStatement>>.
- Hot path (no VALUES subqueries): returns Cow::Borrowed(insert).
- Slow path (subquery rewrite): returns Cow::Owned(new_insert) — same
  cost as before.
- Updated the two callers to use Cow; codegen_insert now takes
  insert.as_ref(). No semantic change; the Cow variant is invisible to
  downstream code which only reads fields.

Build verified via `rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-pager -p fsqlite-core
-p fsqlite-e2e --profile release-perf`. Two unrelated pre-existing test
failures in `connection::pager_routing_tests` (BusySnapshot on SELECT
COUNT during large-text insert) reproduce on clean HEAD and are not
caused by these changes.

Refs: tests/artifacts/perf/profiling-handoff-20260423T155542Z/hotspot-table.md
(T2, T7), session_2026_04_19_campaign_wave2.md
Hot-path evidence: tests/artifacts/perf/bd-m4s2c-20260423T013614Z/
perf-report-mt_writers_{8,16}x500.txt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -4689,6 +4689,17 @@ where
         Ok(self.cache.metrics_snapshot())
     }
 
+    /// Capture cheap cache counters for hot-path statistical sampling.
+    ///
+    /// Skips per-slot iteration and the eviction-policy mutex. Suitable for
+    /// callers that only need hit/miss/admit/evict counts and resident-page
+    /// totals (e.g. the e-process oracle refresh loop).
+    pub fn cache_metrics_lightweight_snapshot(
+        &self,
+    ) -> Result<crate::page_cache::PageCacheLightweightSnapshot> {
+        Ok(self.cache.metrics_lightweight_snapshot())
+    }
+
     /// Capture a read-only snapshot of the resident page-cache entries.
     pub fn cache_page_snapshots(&self) -> Result<Vec<PageCachePageSnapshot>> {
         Ok(self.cache.page_snapshots())
